established_analysts.name: Andy Katz
established_analysts.video_title: Final March Madness men's bracket predictions before Selection Sunday
key_insights: Florida grabbed the 4th No. 1 seed, replacing UConn which dropped to a No. 2 seed
Duke is the No. 1 overall seed in his latest projection
Florida won the SEC by 3 games and is on a heater — winners of 12 of last 13, all but 3 by double digits
Michigan State climbed to a No. 2 seed line in March 3 update
UConn dropped from No. 1 to No. 2 seed line in final update
Katz's Power 37 final rankings released March 9 provide additional context on team strength

additional_videos.title: March Madness bracket predictions: Top seed lines projected on March 3

established_analysts.name: Joe Lunardi
key_insights: Lunardi projects Wisconsin as a No. 6 seed despite 4 wins against top-10 KenPom teams
Wisconsin scored over 90 points in wins over Michigan, Michigan State, Illinois, and Purdue
SEC bubble teams Missouri, Texas, and Auburn are clinging to at-large spots as of March 12
Seton Hall barely clinging to bubble at 13% at-large probability after Big East semifinal win over Creighton
Conference tournament results are rapidly shifting bubble positions
Lunardi's Bracketology watch guide provides real-time updates on how results impact the projected bracket
established_analysts.note: Lunardi primarily publishes via ESPN Bracketology tool and articles; video content embedded in ESPN platform rather than standalone YouTube

fresh_takes.name: XbotGo / Sports Analytics Blog
hot_takes: 12-over-5 upset: South Florida is the most defensible data-backed choice — analytics rate them higher than UCF (projected 10-seed) in underlying efficiency metrics
Miami (Ohio) at 31-0 is a legitimate Cinderella threat — nine different players have scored 20+ points in a game, 40% from three, 62% from two
High Point over UNC is live — UNC lost Caleb Wilson to season-ending hand injury and High Point plays pressure full-court man defense that creates chaos
Nebraska to the second weekend — 26-5 under Hoiberg, best season in program history, never won an NCAA Tournament game, five players avg 9+ points
Skip the 12-over-5 obsession — the smarter play is backing 11-seeds, which went 10-10 over last 5 tournaments with three 11-seeds making Round of 32 in both 2023 and 2024
TCU over BYU is a bracket-buster pick
Vanderbilt is vulnerable despite a 16-0 start — they've been sliding since January
contrarian_picks: Miami (Ohio) — undefeated MAC team as Cinderella
High Point over UNC — targeting depleted roster
fresh_takes.reasoning: Analytics-first approach focusing on underlying efficiency metrics, injury context, and historical upset vectors rather than brand-name bias. Targets teams whose records mask underlying quality (or fragility).

fresh_takes.name: SportsBrackets.net AI Model
fresh_takes.article_title: 2026 March Madness AI Bracket Predictions: Data-Driven Projections & Sleepers
hot_takes: AI model ignores 'blue blood' status and TV narratives — focuses exclusively on Adjusted Efficiency, ShotQuality, and Historical Upset Vectors
Model identified four 'Red Zone' matchups — one per region — where lower seed has statistically significant path to victory
The 'Izzo Factor' is built into the model — historical weighting for elite coaching performance in tournament second weekend
ShotQuality filter separates teams that won through luck vs. sustainable offensive systems
AI-driven models typically outperform human experts by removing emotional bias and anchoring in multi-year efficiency data
contrarian_picks: Four 'Red Zone' matchups identified (specific teams behind paywall/article)
Teams with high ShotQuality but lower seeds are model favorites for upsets
fresh_takes.reasoning: Pure data-driven approach using Adjusted Efficiency + ShotQuality + Historical Upset Vectors. Removes brand bias. Updates in real-time as conference tournament results shift efficiency ratings.

additional_analysis_sources.name: PoolGenius Champion Formula
additional_analysis_sources.key_insight: Only 6 teams fit the historical champion formula — champions have hit 50 of 54 statistical benchmarks. Louisville is the biggest surprise inclusion.
additional_analysis_sources.criteria: Must rank near top nationally in both offensive and defensive efficiency while playing an elite schedule

consensus_summary.consensus_narrative: The 2026 tournament is top-heavy with Duke, Michigan, and Arizona as clear Tier 1. Florida is the hottest team entering the tournament (12-1 in last 13, defending champion). The contrarian play is targeting 11-seeds over 12-over-5 upsets. Wisconsin and West Virginia are the most commonly cited sleepers across sources. Louisville's inclusion in the champion formula is the biggest analytical surprise.

methodology_notes.note: YouTube-specific video results were limited; most bracket analysis content from established analysts (Katz, Lunardi) is hosted on NCAA.com and ESPN platforms respectively, which syndicate to YouTube. Fresh takes were found primarily from sports analytics blogs and AI projection sites rather than independent YouTube creators. All data points are sourced from web search results of published articles and videos.
methodology_notes.limitation: Selection Sunday is March 15, 2026 — the actual 68-team bracket has not yet been revealed. All projections are pre-Selection Sunday estimates. Conference tournaments are still in progress as of March 12.
